title: Robustly Flavored Chinese Basil Chicken Sweet And Sour
description: A citrusy and molten Chinese dish featuring basil, cooked in a papillote for a lazy Sunday, macrobiotic-friendly and sweet and sour in flavor.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together soy sauce, brown sugar, and cornstarch.
Add the chicken to the bowl and toss to coat with the soy sauce mixture.
Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the chicken to the skillet and cook until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger to the skillet and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the sweet and sour sauce and bring to a simmer.
Place the chicken and sauce mixture onto the parchment paper, topping with chopped Chinese basil.
Fold the parchment paper to create a papillote and bake for 15 minutes.
Serve the chicken and sauce hot, garnished with additional Chinese basil if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
